title: Gramática Esencial en Diferentes Idiomas
text:
1 Bienvenida
Este es el libro web creado con Quarto. Se está preparando lecciones básicas para aprender grammar en inglés, alemán, frances e italiano

Unit1: Word forms and BE verb

English - Be verb
German - SEIN
French - Présent de être
Italian - Presente di essere

title: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb
text:
2.1 English - Be verb
There are differtent kinds of words in a language.
nouns: A noun is a person, place or thing e.g. mother, teacher, baby, city, classroom, pen chair. There are diferent types of nouns
verbs: A verb is an action word e.g. cook, drink, laugh, dance
adjectives: An adjective is a word that describes a noun e.g. tall, hot, pink.
Be connects the subject to information about the subject. It isn’t an action word.
Tha main structure is —> subject + BE + adjective
crumbs: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb

title: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb
text:
Subject (Person)
Form of to be (+ / -)
Example




I
am / am not
I am happy. (Estoy feliz.)


you (informal/formal, singular)
are / are not (aren’t)
You are nice. (Eres amable.)


he
is / is not (isn’t)
He is small. (Él es pequeño.)


she
is / is not (isn’t)
She is tired. (Ella está cansada.)


it
is / is not (isn’t)
It is cold. (Hace frío.)


we
are / are not (aren’t)
We are here. (Estamos aquí.)


you (plural)
are / are not (aren’t)
You are late. (Ustedes están retrasados.)


they
are / are not (aren’t)
They are loud. (Ellos son ruidosos.)





This is Jenifer (Esta es Jenifer.)
She is five years old
She has a brother
He is seven years old
What’s your name? –> My name is Lucas
I’m German. I’m from Berlin
I’m a student
My father is a Doctor and my mother is an engineer
My favourite color is blue
I’m interested in music
I’m not tired
He isn’t here
We aren’t students
I’m tired but I’m not hungry
The weather is nice today
Lucas is not at home, he is at work
Ana and I are good friends
It’s ten o’clock. Your’e late again
Pablo is not a teacher. He is a lawyer
My hands are cold


2.1.1 Practice conversations
Conversation1: Meeting someone new
A: Good day! I am Ms. Keller. What is your name?
B: Good day, Ms. Keller. I am Mr. Fischer.
A: Are you from Germany?
B: No, I am not from Germany. I am from Austria. And you?
A: I am from Berlin.
B: Nice to meet you!
Conversation 2 — Talking to a friend
A: Hello! How are you? Are you tired?
B: Yes, I’m a little tired. And you?
A: No, I’m not tired; I’m hungry!
B: We’re both at the café. The food here is good.
A: Yes, you’re right!
Conversation 3 — Describing family
A: Is that your brother?
B: Yes, that’s my brother. He is seventeen years old.
A: And who is that?
B: That’s my sister. She is a university student.
A: Are you from Munich?
B: No, we are from Hamburg.


2.1.2 Exercise – Choose the Correct Form of Verb
Instructions: Choose the correct answer (am, is, or are) to complete each sentence.

Apples …. healthy [ am / is / are ]. (Las manzanas son saludables.)
Dogs …. good pets [ am / is / are ]. (Los perros son buenas mascotas.)
Melissa …. sick [ am / is / are ]. (Melissa está enferma.)
Alex …. a helicopter pilot [ am / is / are ]. (Alex es piloto de helicóptero.)
The man …. poor [ am / is / are ]. (El hombre es pobre.)
I …. at school now [ am / is / are ]. (Ahora estoy en la escuela.)
We …. happy today [ am / is / are ]. (Hoy estamos felices.)
Judy and Robert …. police officers [ am / is / are ]. (Judy y Robert son policías.)
The cat …. sleepy [ am / is / are ]. (El gato tiene sueño.)
She …. at home [ am / is / are ]. (Ella está en casa.)
The tigers …. hungry [ am / is / are ]. (Los tigres tienen hambre.)
I …. from Canada [ am / is / are ]. (Soy de Canadá.)
Those men …. bus drivers [ am / is / are ]. (Esos hombres son conductores de autobús.)
Those shoes …. from Brazil [ am / is / are ]. (Esos zapatos son de Brasil.)
We …. at the bank [ am / is / are ]. (Estamos en el banco.)
The piano …. heavy [ am / is / are ]. (El piano es pesado.)
That hamburger …. American [ am / is / are ]. (Esa hamburguesa es estadounidense.)
You …. an artist [ am / is / are ]. (Eres un artista.)
The red cabinet …. from China [ am / is / are ]. (El gabinete rojo es de China.)
You …. good students [ am / is / are ]. (Ustedes son buenos estudiantes.)
crumbs: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb

title: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb
text:
2.2 German - SEIN
EINHEIT 1: bin / bist / ist / sind / seid / sind (Präsens von sein)








Subject (Person)
Form of sein (+ / −)
Example




ich (I)
bin / bin nicht
Ich bin glücklich. (I am happy.)


du (you – informal, singular)
bist / bist nicht
Du bist nett. (You are nice.)


er / sie / es (he / she / it)
ist / ist nicht
Er ist klein. (He is small.)


wir (we)
sind / sind nicht
Wir sind hier. (We are here.)


ihr (you – informal, plural)
seid / seid nicht
Ihr seid spät. (You are late.)


sie (they)
sind / sind nicht
Sie sind laut. (They are loud.)


Sie (you – formal, singular/plural)
sind / sind nicht
Sie sind willkommen. (You are welcome.)




Das ist Jennifer. (This is Jennifer.)
Sie ist fünf Jahre alt. (She is five years old.)
Sie hat einen Bruder. (She has a brother.)
Er ist sieben Jahre alt. (He is seven years old.)
Wie heißt du? – Ich heiße Lucas. (What’s your name? – My name is Lucas.)
Ich bin Deutscher. Ich komme aus Berlin. (I’m German. I’m from Berlin.)
> Note: If the speaker is female: Ich bin Deutsche.
Ich bin Student. (I’m a student.)
> Note: If the speaker is female: Ich bin Studentin.
Mein Vater ist Arzt und meine Mutter ist Ingenieurin. (My father is a doctor and my mother is an engineer.)
Meine Lieblingsfarbe ist Blau. (My favourite color is blue.)
Ich bin interessiert an Musik (I’m interested in music.)
Ich bin nicht müde. (I’m not tired.)
Er ist nicht hier. (He isn’t here.)
Wir sind keine Studenten. (We aren’t students.)
Ich bin müde, aber ich habe keinen Hunger. (I’m tired, but I’m not hungry.)
Das Wetter ist heute schön. (The weather is nice today.)
Lucas ist nicht zu Hause. Er ist bei der Arbeit. (Lucas is not at home. He is at work.)
Ana und ich sind gute Freunde. (Ana and I are good friends.)
Es ist zehn Uhr. Du bist schon wieder zu spät. (It’s ten o’clock. You’re late again.)
Pablo ist kein Lehrer. Er ist Anwalt. (Pablo is not a teacher. He is a lawyer.)
Meine Hände sind kalt. (My hands are cold.)


2.2.1 Practice conversations

2.2.1.1 Conversation 1 — Meeting someone new (formal, using Sie)

A: Guten Tag! Ich bin Frau Keller. Wie ist Ihr Name? (A: Good day! I am Ms. Keller. What is your name?)
B: Guten Tag, Frau Keller. Ich bin Herr Fischer. (B: Good day, Ms. Keller. I am Mr. Fischer.)
A: Sind Sie aus Deutschland? (A: Are you from Germany?)
B: Nein, ich bin nicht aus Deutschland. Ich bin aus Österreich. Und Sie? (B: No, I am not from Germany. I am from Austria. And you?)
A: Ich bin aus Berlin. (A: I am from Berlin.)
B: Schön, Sie kennenzulernen! (B: Nice to meet you!)



2.2.1.2 Conversation 2 — Talking to a friend (informal, using du)

A: Hallo! Wie geht’s? Bist du müde? (A: Hello! How are you? Are you tired?)
B: Ja, ich bin ein bisschen müde. Und du? (B: Yes, I’m a little tired. And you?)
A: Nein, ich bin nicht müde, ich bin hungrig! (A: No, I’m not tired; I’m hungry!)
B: Wir sind beide im Café. Das Essen hier ist gut. (B: We’re both at the café. The food here is good.)
A: Ja, du hast recht! (A: Yes, you’re right!)



2.2.1.3 Conversation 3 — Describing family (mixed practice)

A: Ist das dein Bruder? (A: Is that your brother?)
B: Ja, das ist mein Bruder. Er ist siebzehn Jahre alt. (B: Yes, that’s my brother. He is seventeen years old.)
A: Und wer ist das? (A: And who is that?)
B: Das ist meine Schwester. Sie ist Studentin. (B: That’s my sister. She is a university student.)
A: Seid ihr aus München? (A: Are you from Munich?)
B: Nein, wir sind aus Hamburg. (B: No, we are from Hamburg.)




2.2.2 Übung – Wähle die richtige Form von SEIN (Ejercicio – Elige la forma correcta del verbo “to be”)
Anleitung: Wähle die richtige Antwort (bin, ist oder sind), um jeden Satz zu vervollständigen.

Äpfel …. gesund [ bin / ist / sind ]. (Apples are healthy.)
Hunde …. gute Haustiere [ bin / ist / sind ]. (Dogs are good pets.)
Melissa …. krank [ bin / ist / sind ]. (Melissa is sick.)
Alex …. Hubschrauberpilot [ bin / ist / sind ]. (Alex is a helicopter pilot.)
Der Mann …. arm [ bin / ist / sind ]. (The man is poor.)
Ich …. jetzt in der Schule [ bin / ist / sind ]. (I am at school now.)
Wir …. heute glücklich [ bin / ist / sind ]. (We are happy today.)
Judy und Robert …. Polizisten [ bin / ist / sind ]. (Judy and Robert are police officers.)
Die Katze …. schläfrig [ bin / ist / sind ]. (The cat is sleepy.)
Sie …. zu Hause [ bin / ist / sind ]. (She is at home.)
Die Tiger …. hungrig [ bin / ist / sind ]. (The tigers are hungry.)
Ich …. aus Kanada [ bin / ist / sind ]. (I am from Canada.)
Diese Männer …. Busfahrer [ bin / ist / sind ]. (Those men are bus drivers.)
Diese Schuhe …. aus Brasilien [ bin / ist / sind ]. (Those shoes are from Brazil.)
Wir …. in der Bank [ bin / ist / sind ]. (We are at the bank.)
Das Klavier …. schwer [ bin / ist / sind ]. (The piano is heavy.)
Dieser Hamburger …. amerikanisch [ bin / ist / sind ]. (That hamburger is American.)
Du …. Künstler [ bin / bist / sind ]. (You are an artist.)
Der rote Schrank …. aus China [ bin / ist / sind ]. (The red cabinet is from China.)
Ihr …. gute Schüler [ bin / ist / seid ]. (You are good students.)
crumbs: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb

title: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb
text:
2.3 French - Présent de être
UNITÉ 1 : suis / es / est / sommes / êtes / sont (Basic Sentences with être (to be))








Subject (Person)
Form of être (+ / −)
Example




je (I)
suis / ne suis pas
Je suis heureux. (I am happy.)


tu (you – informal, singular)
es / n’es pas
Tu es gentil. (You are nice.)


il / elle / on (he / she / it / one)
est / n’est pas
Il est petit. (He is small.)


nous (we)
sommes / ne sommes pas
Nous sommes ici. (We are here.)


vous (you – informal/formal, plural; formal, singular)
êtes / n’êtes pas
Vous êtes en retard. (You are late.)


ils / elles (they)
sont / ne sont pas
Ils sont bruyants. (They are loud.)


vous (you – formal, singular/plural)
êtes / n’êtes pas
Vous êtes les bienvenus. (You are welcome.)




Voici Jennifer. (This is Jennifer.)
Elle a cinq ans. (She is five years old.)
Elle a un frère. (She has a brother.)
Il a sept ans. (He is seven years old.)
Quel est ton nom? – Mon nom est Lucas. (What’s your name? – My name is Lucas.)
Je suis allemand(e). Je viens de Berlin. (I’m German. I’m from Berlin.)
Je suis étudiant(e). (I’m a student.)
Mon père est médecin et ma mère est ingénieure. (My father is a doctor and my mother is an engineer.)
Ma couleur préférée est le bleu. (My favourite color is blue.)
Je suis intéresse par la musique. (I’m interested in music.)
Je ne suis pas fatigué(e). (I’m not tired.)
Il n’est pas ici. (He isn’t here.)
Nous ne sommes pas étudiants. (We aren’t students.)
Je suis fatigué(e), mais je n’ai pas faim. (I’m tired, but I’m not hungry.)
Le temps est beau aujourd’hui. (The weather is nice today.)
Lucas n’est pas à la maison. Il est au travail. (Lucas is not at home. He is at work.)
Ana et moi sommes de bons amis. (Ana and I are good friends.)
Il est dix heures. Tu es encore en retard. (It’s ten o’clock. You’re late again.)
Pablo n’est pas professeur. Il est avocat. (Pablo is not a teacher. He is a lawyer.)
J’ai les mains froides. (My hands are cold.)



2.3.1 Practice Conversations

2.3.1.1 Conversation 1 – Meeting Someone New
A: Bonjour ! Je suis Madame Keller. Comment vous appelez-vous ?
(Good day! I am Ms. Keller. What is your name?)
B: Bonjour, Madame Keller. Je suis Monsieur Fischer.
(Good day, Ms. Keller. I am Mr. Fischer.)
A: Vous êtes d’Allemagne ?
(Are you from Germany?)
B: Non, je ne suis pas d’Allemagne. Je suis d’Autriche. Et vous ?
(No, I am not from Germany. I am from Austria. And you?)
A: Je suis de Berlin.
(I am from Berlin.)
B: Enchanté(e) de faire votre connaissance !
(Nice to meet you!)



2.3.1.2 Conversation 2 – Talking to a Friend
A: Salut ! Comment ça va ? Tu es fatigué(e) ?
(Hello! How are you? Are you tired?)
B: Oui, je suis un peu fatigué(e). Et toi ?
(Yes, I’m a little tired. And you?)
A: Non, je ne suis pas fatigué(e) ; j’ai faim !
(No, I’m not tired; I’m hungry!)
B: Nous sommes tous les deux au café. La nourriture est bonne ici.
(We’re both at the café. The food here is good.)
A: Oui, tu as raison !
(Yes, you’re right!)



2.3.1.3 Conversation 3 – Describing Family
A: C’est ton frère ?
(Is that your brother?)
B: Oui, c’est mon frère. Il a dix-sept ans.
(Yes, that’s my brother. He is seventeen years old.)
A: Et qui est-ce ?
(And who is that?)
B: C’est ma sœur. Elle est étudiante à l’université.
(That’s my sister. She is a university student.)
A: Vous êtes de Munich ?
(Are you from Munich?)
B: Non, nous sommes de Hambourg.
(No, we are from Hamburg.)



2.3.2 Exercice – Choisissez la bonne forme du verbe être
Instructions : Choisissez la bonne réponse (suis, est ou sont) pour compléter correctement chaque phrase.

Les pommes …. bonnes pour la santé [ suis / est / sont ]. (Apples are healthy.)
Les chiens …. de bons animaux de compagnie [ suis / est / sont ]. (Dogs are good pets.)
Melissa …. malade [ suis / est / sont ]. (Melissa is sick.)
Alex …. pilote d’hélicoptère [ suis / est / sont ]. (Alex is a helicopter pilot.)
L’homme …. pauvre [ suis / est / sont ]. (The man is poor.)
Je …. à l’école maintenant [ suis / est / sont ]. (I am at school now.)
Nous …. heureux aujourd’hui [ suis / est / sommes ]. (We are happy today.)
Judy et Robert …. policiers [ suis / est / sont ]. (Judy and Robert are police officers.)
Le chat …. fatigué [ suis / est / sont ]. (The cat is sleepy.)
Elle …. à la maison [ suis / est / sont ]. (She is at home.)
Les tigres …. affamés [ suis / est / sont ]. (The tigers are hungry.)
Je …. du Canada [ suis / est / sont ]. (I am from Canada.)
Ces hommes …. chauffeurs de bus [ suis / est / sont ]. (Those men are bus drivers.)
Ces chaussures …. du Brésil [ suis / est / sont ]. (Those shoes are from Brazil.)
Nous …. à la banque [ suis / est / sommes ]. (We are at the bank.)
Le piano …. lourd [ suis / est / sont ]. (The piano is heavy.)
Ce hamburger …. américain [ suis / est / sont ]. (That hamburger is American.)
Tu …. artiste [ suis / es / sont ]. (You are an artist.)
L’armoire rouge …. de Chine [ suis / est / sont ]. (The red cabinet is from China.)
Vous …. de bons étudiants [ suis / est / êtes ]. (You are good students.)
crumbs: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb

title: 2  Unit1: Word forms and BE verb
text:
2.4 Italian - Presente di essere
UNITÀ 1: sono / sei / è / siamo / siete / sono (Basic Sentences with essere (to be))








Soggetto (Persona)
Forma di essere (+ / −)
Esempio




io (I)
sono / non sono
Io sono felice. (I am happy.)


tu (you – informal, singular)
sei / non sei
Tu sei gentile. (You are nice.)


lui / lei (he / she)
è / non è
Lui è piccolo. (He is small.)


noi (we)
siamo / non siamo
Noi siamo qui. (We are here.)


voi (you – informal, plural)
siete / non siete
Voi siete in ritardo. (You are late.)


loro (they)
sono / non sono
Loro sono rumorosi. (They are loud.)


Lei (you – formal, singular)
è / non è
Lei è il benvenuto / la benvenuta. (You are welcome.)




Questa è Jennifer. (This is Jennifer.)
Lei ha cinque anni. (She is five years old.)
Lei ha un fratello. (She has a brother.)
Lui ha sette anni. (He is seven years old.)
Come ti chiami? – Mi chiamo Lucas. (What’s your name? – My name is Lucas.)
Sono tedesco/tedesca. Vengo da Berlino. (I’m German. I’m from Berlin.)
Sono uno studente / una studentessa. (I’m a student.)
Mio padre è medico e mia madre è ingegnera. (My father is a doctor and my mother is an engineer.)
Il mio colore preferito è il blu. (My favourite color is blue.)
Mi interessa la musica. (I’m interested in music.)
Non sono stanco/stanca. (I’m not tired.)
Lui non è qui. (He isn’t here.)
Noi non siamo studenti. (We aren’t students.)
Sono stanco/stanca, ma non ho fame. (I’m tired, but I’m not hungry.)
Oggi il tempo è bello. (The weather is nice today.)
Lucas non è a casa. È al lavoro. (Lucas is not at home. He is at work.)
Ana e io siamo buoni amici. (Ana and I are good friends.)
Sono le dieci. Sei di nuovo in ritardo. (It’s ten o’clock. You’re late again.)
Pablo non è un insegnante. È un avvocato. (Pablo is not a teacher. He is a lawyer.)
Le mie mani sono fredde. (My hands are cold.)



2.4.1 Practice Conversations

2.4.1.1 Conversation 1 – Meeting Someone New
A: Buongiorno! Sono la signora Keller. Come si chiama?
(Good day! I am Ms. Keller. What is your name?)
B: Buongiorno, signora Keller. Sono il signor Fischer.
(Good day, Ms. Keller. I am Mr. Fischer.)
A: È della Germania?
(Are you from Germany?)
B: No, non sono della Germania. Sono dell’Austria. E Lei?
(No, I am not from Germany. I am from Austria. And you?)
A: Vengo da Berlino.
(I am from Berlin.)
B: Piacere di conoscerLa!
(Nice to meet you!)



2.4.1.2 Conversation 2 – Talking to a Friend
A: Ciao! Come stai? Sei stanco/stanca?
(Hello! How are you? Are you tired?)
B: Sì, sono un po’ stanco/stanca. E tu?
(Yes, I’m a little tired. And you?)
A: No, non sono stanco/stanca; ho fame!
(No, I’m not tired; I’m hungry!)
B: Siamo tutti e due al bar. Il cibo qui è buono.
(We’re both at the café. The food here is good.)
A: Sì, hai ragione!
(Yes, you’re right!)



2.4.1.3 Conversation 3 – Describing Family
A: È tuo fratello?
(Is that your brother?)
B: Sì, è mio fratello. Ha diciassette anni.
(Yes, that’s my brother. He is seventeen years old.)
A: E chi è quella?
(And who is that?)
B: È mia sorella. È una studentessa universitaria.
(That’s my sister. She is a university student.)
A: Siete di Monaco?
(Are you from Munich?)
B: No, siamo di Amburgo.
(No, we are from Hamburg.)



2.4.2 Esercizio – Scegli la forma corretta del verbo essere
Istruzioni: Scegli la risposta corretta (sono, è o sono) per completare correttamente ogni frase.

Le mele …. sane [ sono / è / sono ]. (Apples are healthy.)
I cani …. buoni animali domestici [ sono / è / sono ]. (Dogs are good pets.)
Melissa …. malata [ sono / è / sono ]. (Melissa is sick.)
Alex …. pilota di elicottero [ sono / è / sono ]. (Alex is a helicopter pilot.)
L’uomo …. povero [ sono / è / sono ]. (The man is poor.)
Io …. a scuola adesso [ sono / è / sono ]. (I am at school now.)
Noi …. felici oggi [ sono / è / siamo ]. (We are happy today.)
Judy e Robert …. poliziotti [ sono / è / sono ]. (Judy and Robert are police officers.)
Il gatto …. assonnato [ sono / è / sono ]. (The cat is sleepy.)
Lei …. a casa [ sono / è / sono ]. (She is at home.)
Le tigri …. affamate [ sono / è / sono ]. (The tigers are hungry.)
Io …. del Canada [ sono / è / sono ]. (I am from Canada.)
Quegli uomini …. autisti di autobus [ sono / è / sono ]. (Those men are bus drivers.)
Quelle scarpe …. del Brasile [ sono / è / sono ]. (Those shoes are from Brazil.)
Noi …. in banca [ sono / è / siamo ]. (We are at the bank.)
Il pianoforte …. pesante [ sono / è / sono ]. (The piano is heavy.)
Quell’hamburger …. americano [ sono / è / sono ]. (That hamburger is American.)
Tu …. un artista [ sono / sei / sono ]. (You are an artist.)
L’armadio rosso …. della Cina [ sono / è / sono ]. (The red cabinet is from China.)
Voi …. bravi studenti [ sono / è / siete ]. (You are good students.)

title: 3  Capítulo: Intro to Data Science with Python
crumbs: 3  Capítulo: Intro to Data Science with Python

title: 3  Capítulo: Intro to Data Science with Python
text:
3.1.1 Reading and Writing Files with Open
File handling is an essential aspect of programming, and Python provides built-in functions to interact with files. This guide will explore how to use Python’s open function to read the text files (‘.txt’ files).
There are two methods for opening the file using the file handling concept.
crumbs: 3  Capítulo: Intro to Data Science with Python

title: 4  Capítulo: SQL fundamentals
text:
4.1 Introduction to Databases and SQL
Welcome to the “SQL Fundamentals” course! In today’s world, data is a critical business asset. But, if we can’t tap into that data, if we can’t access and manipulate it, it’s wasted. SQL solves that problem. It’s a language of databases. It allows you to communicate with a database and get what you need. It’s a critical skill in every analyst or data scientist’s toolbox, and it appears on most job postings for those positions. You’re going to develop that skill within this course. By the end, you will learn to construct a wide variety of SQL statements. By the end, you will learn to construct a wide variety of SQL statements. We start with the basics, and then move to more advanced concepts – like joins, subqueries, common table expressions, and window functions. Finally, you’ll also learn how to optimize your SQL statements so they’re efficient even on extremely large data sets.
Review the Course Learning Outcomes
By the end of this course, you’ll be able to…
Learning Objectives:
By the end of this section, you’ll be able to…

title: 5  Capítulo: Basic Statistics Using Python

title: 5  Capítulo: Basic Statistics Using Python
text:
5.1.1 Overview
Learning Objectives:
By the end of this section, you’ll be able to…

Calculate descriptive statistics.
Identify and create appropriate graphs for given situations.
Practice cleaning a dataset.
crumbs: 5  Capítulo: Basic Statistics Using Python

title: 6  Capítulo: Probability for Computer Science
text:
6.1 Introduction to Probability
The idea of supply chains is managing flows. And flows of what? Flows of product or material, flows of information, and flows of money.
Course Overview and Basic Tools
starts by providing a fundamental grounding in combinatorics, and then quickly moves into the basics of probability theory. We will then cover many essential concepts in probability theory, including particular probability distributions, properties of probabilities, and mathematical tools for analyzing probabilities. Finally, the last third of the class will focus on data analysis and machine learning as a means for seeing direct applications of probability in this exciting and quickly growing subfield of computer science.
crumbs: 6  Capítulo: Probability for Computer Science

title: 7  Data Visualization and Cloud Technologies
text:
In this course, we learn to use data visualization and cloud computing to support business analytics. In the cloud computing section, we use the cloud to build scalable data warehouses, analyze big data, and develop machine learning models. In the data visualization section, we create and share compelling visualizations that enhance decision-making.
In the first half of the course, you’ll learn how to tell compelling stories using data visualization. Then in the second half, we’ll learning about cloud computing. You’ll set up and manage a cloud data warehouse, build and deploy a machine learning model in the cloud, and analyze big data in a cloud environment. These skills will prepare you to add value to your career right away.
crumbs: 7  Data Visualization and Cloud Technologies

title: 8  Capítulo: Data Science with R
text:
8.1 Data Science
Data science is a multidisciplinary field that uses scientific methods, processes, algorithms and systems to extract knowledge and insights from structured and unstructured data. It combines aspects of statistics, computer science, and domain expertise to analyze and interpret complex data sets. Data scientists use various tools and techniques to clean, process, and analyze data, often employing machine learning algorithms to make predictions or uncover patterns. The goal of data science is to turn data into actionable insights that can inform decision-making and drive business strategies. It is widely used in various industries, including finance, healthcare, marketing, and technology, to solve complex problems and improve outcomes.
crumbs: 8  Capítulo: Data Science with R
